page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-88 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 88 vCPUs mapped to 44 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core and a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 352.0 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, remains in the average middle 50% tier.

Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong performance in the top 25% for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes. The instance achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for Geekbench image processing. It also registers strong top 25% performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and software compilation with Clang. For large language model inference, prompt processing speeds for small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top 25%, while text generation speeds range from average to strong depending on model size.

From a resource density perspective, the c3-standard-88 offers high compute density with dedicated CPU allocation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that can leverage its 44 physical cores and advanced instruction sets. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, requiring external network-attached storage and limiting its suitability for heavy GPU-accelerated deep learning training. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-88 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 88 vCPUs across 44 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores and 352.0 GB of dedicated RAM. Operating at 2.7 GHz, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng and PassMark tests, alongside top 10% performance in image processing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached and uncached read/write operations. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, presenting a tradeoff for storage-heavy or GPU-dependent tasks. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads, making it suitable for software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server with 88 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier image processing capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-88 features 88 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A high-density, x86_64 server featuring 88 dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads and CPU-based inference.
bullet_points: 88 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for matrix operations
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Top-tier performance in image processing and static web serving
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
